rulings.Case Title: DONALD VOGEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 17, 2008, line 12. DEFENDANT CARRIER CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted based on the affirmative defense of military contractor immunity. The Horne Declaration is admissible for the foundation has been established for each of the points made. The Ay Declaration fails to create a triable issue of fact. It is the Court's conclusion that plaintiff has failed to demonstrate that Ay is qualified to testify to the opinions expressed in paragraphs 4 and 5, in particular paragraph 5, page 4: 9-11. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STANDAERT VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 17, 2008, line 13. DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary adjudication is granted on punitive damages. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence based on plaintiff's response to special interrogatories. It does not appear that the exhibits to the Judd declaration are admissible for lack of foundation. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT PECHAR VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 17, 2008, line 14. DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence and plaintiff has not presented evidence of a triable issue of fact. The record reflects only that plaintiff worked with Flexitallic gaskets at Mare Island but plaintiff has no idea from whom the Navy obtained the gaskets (undisputed material fact 14). There is no evidence to link Power or Lammons to the gaskets purchased by the Navy for use at Mare Island. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: WARREN HARRINGTON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Require Warren Harrington To Post A Cost Bond; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Roger P. Downes
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 17, 2008, line 17. DEFENDANT COOPER CAMERON CORPORATION'S Motion To Require Warren Harrington To Post A Cost Bond is denied. Defendant has failed to demonstrate that it can present any admissible evidence to rebut plaintiff's testimony. The Johnson Declaration is devoid of foundational facts. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA SCHAEFER et al VS. WILBUR WOO INDIVIDUALLY AND AS THE TRUSTEE OF et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, OCTOBER 17, 2008. LINE 18. DEFENDANTS CLARKE AND COMPANY INVESTIGATIONS AND SHELLEY CLARKE INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA CLARKE AND COMPANY INVESTIGATIONS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: REA RENE LAMPARAS-MCCULLOGH VS. PENSON INC (DBA PENSON TRUST COMPANY) et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 17, 2008, line 19. DEFENDANT JAMES GUCKIAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant has failed to shift the burden of producing evidence. The motion for summary adjudication is denied for failure to shift the burden of producing evidence. Assuming arguendo the burden was shifted, plaintiff's declaration creates a triable issue of fact. Plaintiff presents admissible evidence relevant to her state of mind as well as admissible to prove the conduct at issue. =(302/PJM)
